output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Your Step-by-Step Recovery Guide
output.meta_description: Your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your rights, legal deadlines, and how to demand your money back with a free demand letter tool.
content_outline: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? You're Not Alone
Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit? Understanding State Deadlines
What Can a Landlord Deduct From Your Security Deposit (And What's Illegal)
What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit: A 3-Step Action Plan
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Actually Works
When to Escalate: Small Claims Court and Tenant Rights
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14–60 days and provide an itemized deduction list.
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; only excessive damage and unpaid rent are typically legal deductions.
Tenants have the right to dispute wrongful deductions with a formal, state-compliant demand letter.
DepositBack automates legally sound demand letters to recover deposits quickly without hiring a lawyer.
output.cta: Stop waiting and start recovering. Use DepositBack to create a legally sound security deposit demand letter in minutes—no attorney, no stress, no more excuses from your landlord.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware U.S. renters searching for help after their landlord withheld, delayed, or refused to return a security deposit.
